scenario: You are configuring a Custom API message named 'Custom API Example' using the plug-in registration tool, as shown in the exhibit. It is configured as an Action (not a Function), it is not marked as Private, and it runs plug-in logic that creates contact records. You need to evaluate the statements below.
q: For each statement, select Yes if it is true, otherwise No.

rows.s: You must configure at least one response property.
'Custom API Example' will appear in the $metadata service document.
If the calling user does not have the permissions to create contact records, they will receive an error.

why: Response properties are NOT mandatory: because this Custom API is an Action (it performs an action) rather than a Function (which retrieves data), the POST request does not have to return anything, so you do not need to configure a response property (statement 1 = No). Because the Custom API is not marked as Private, its name 'Custom API Example' WILL appear in the $metadata service document (…/api/data/v9.2/$metadata) and be usable by code-generation tools (statement 2 = Yes). The Custom API's plug-in runs in the CONTEXT of the calling user, so if that user lacks the Create privilege on contact, the create fails and they receive an error (statement 3 = Yes).
